tools.name: Directions array to visit grid neighbors
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to write clean code to iterate over grid neighbors by using a directions array.

tools.name: Factoring out validation to helper functions
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to simplify the main logic by moving complex checks into helper functions - like boundary checks in grids.

tools.name: Implementing subgrid traversals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to iterate cleanly over arbitrary subgrids.

tools.name: Handling wraparounds with mod
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to simplify code by using modulo arithmetic to cycle through options.

tools.name: Reusing subproblems
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to identify overlapping subproblems and reuse their solutions.

tools.name: Rectifying double counting
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to avoid or correct double counting when adding overlapping quantities.

tools.name: Manipulating matrices
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to do basic matrix operations - like rotate, transpose, and reflect.

tools.name: Inward pointers
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use two pointers that move inward from the ends toward the center.

tools.name: Fast and slow pointers
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use two pointers moving at different speeds through an array.

tools.name: Parallel pointers
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use two pointers across two arrays.

tools.name: Selecting the best iteration order
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to choose the iteration order that simplifies the logic the most - nobody forces us to always go left to right.

tools.name: Working with intervals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being comfortable with problems where the input consists of intervals.

tools.name: In-place modification
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to modify arrays in place without extra space.

tools.name: Seeker and writer pointers
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to use two pointers for in-place array modification - one seeking elements to write and the other writing them.

tools.name: Searching with inward pointers
other_problems: Count Pairs with Sum Greater Than Target
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to search with two inward-moving pointers when the extremes inform each other - when we can discard the smallest element based on the largest one or vice versa.

tools.name: Invariants
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to define and maintain invariants to guide tricky implementations.

tools.name: Prefix-suffix swap trick
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to swap a prefix and a suffix in place in an array - with the triple subarray reversal trick.

tools.name: Transition-point recipe
other_problems: First True In Ascending Boolean Array
tools.wanted_outcome: Having a binary search recipe that works for any binary search problem; the transition-point recipe works by modeling any binary search as finding a transition point from a 'before' region to an 'after' region.

tools.name: Binary search for first and last
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to binary search for the first and last occurrences in arrays with duplicates.

tools.name: Guess-and-check technique
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to binary search for the optimal value of optimization problems.

tools.name: Exponential search
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to find an upper bound for binary search via exponential search.

tools.name: Grid flattening
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to map between 2D grid coordinates and 1D indices.

tools.name: Min-max optimization
tools.wanted_outcome: Being comfortable with problems about minimizing a maximum (or vice versa).

tools.name: Tinkering with dynamic array internals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to understand how dynamic arrays handle resizing internally and apply amortized analysis.

tools.name: Manipulating strings
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement basic string operations like join and split manually.

tools.name: Building strings with dynamic arrays
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to build strings efficiently by appending characters to a dynamic array; important in languages with immutable strings.

tools.name: Rolling hash
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to implement a rolling hash - a hash function that is updated incrementally as a window slides through text.

tools.name: Hash sets
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to store unique elements with expected O(1) access time.

tools.name: Hash maps
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to store key-value pairs with expected O(1) access time.

tools.name: Frequency maps
other_problems: Longest Period At Most K Distinct
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to count occurrences with hash maps.

tools.name: Imposing a canonical order
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to compare (or hash) unsorted collections by first sorting elements into a canonical order.

tools.name: Length-26 arrays
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to use a fixed-size length-26 array as a hashmap alternative when the keys are lowercase letters.

tools.name: Implementing classes
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement a class that satisfies specified operations.

tools.name: Amortized analysis
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use amortized analysis to analyze the average time complexity of data structure operations.

tools.name: Custom sorting logic
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to sort using custom comparators.

tools.name: Sorting to track the smallest/largest elements
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to iterate through the elements of an array from largest to smallest or vice versa without sorting it - by sorting the indices instead.

tools.name: Top k techniques (incl. quickselect)
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to find the top K elements via sorting, heaps, or quickselect.

tools.name: Sorting preprocessing
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to sort up front to simplify or speed up the rest of the solution.

tools.name: Leveraging the input range
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use specialized sorts such as counting sort or bucket sort when the value range is small relative to the number of elements.

tools.name: Counting sort
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing counting sort - a sorting algorithm for when the number of elements is larger than the range of values.

tools.name: Bucket sort
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing bucket sort - a sorting algorithm for sorting objects based on a field with a small range of values.

tools.name: Using stacks for LIFO problems
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve problems requiring LIFO (last-in-first-out) behavior by using a stack.

tools.name: Using queues for FIFO problems
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve problems requiring FIFO (first-in-first-out) behavior by using a queue.

tools.name: Piggybacking extra info
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to store auxiliary fields alongside the primary data in a data structure to simplify future bookkeeping.

tools.name: Reframing balanced parentheses as heights
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to visualize balanced parentheses as a mountain range to unlock easier reasoning.

tools.name: Dangling bracket stack
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to use a stack to track unmatched opening brackets or parentheses.

tools.name: Recursion
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to write and reason about recursive functions.

tools.name: Working with recursive data structures
tools.wanted_outcome: Being comfortable with data structures that contain nested instances of themselves.

tools.name: Passing indices; not copies
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to pass subarrays to functions efficiently - by passing index ranges instead of copying the subarrays.

tools.name: Recurrence relations
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to derive a recurrence (recursive formula) for computing the solution.

tools.name: Divide and conquer
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to break problems into smaller subproblems, solve them recursively, and combine the results.

tools.name: Tinkering with linked-list internals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement linked lists.

tools.name: Linked-list dummy node
other_problems: Remove Kth Node From The End
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to simplify linked-list edge cases by using a dummy head node.

tools.name: Fast and slow pointers (linked lists)
other_problems: Remove Kth Node From The End
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use two pointers moving at different speeds through a linked list.

tools.name: Parallel pointers (linked lists)
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use two pointers across two linked lists.

tools.name: In-place modification (linked lists)
tools.primary_problem: Duplicate Removal In Sorted Linked List
other_problems: Remove Kth Node From The End
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to modify linked lists by rearranging pointers.

tools.name: Cycle detection (linked lists)
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to detect cycles in linked lists using fast and slow pointers.

tools.name: Doubly-linked list
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to work with and implement doubly linked lists.

tools.name: Implementing queues
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement a queue - usually with a with a linked list.

tools.name: Linked-list reversal break down
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve advanced linked-list reversal problems - by breaking them down into 4 steps: (1) finding the ends of the section to reverse, (2) breaking out the section from the main list, (3) reversing the standalone section, and (4) re-attaching the reversed section.

tools.name: Passing data up and down rooted trees
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to decide what information should flow down the tree, up the tree, or bypass recursion in a tree DFS.

tools.name: Pre/in/post-order traversals
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing preorder, inorder, and postorder traversals - a classification of tree DFS traversals based on whether each node is visited before, in between, or after the children.

tools.name: Finding the node in the path with minimum depth
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to use the fact that a path in a rooted tree has a single node with minimum depth.

tools.name: Node-depth queue recipe for level-order traversal
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to traverse a tree level by level while tracking node depths.

tools.name: BST search
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to search efficiently in a binary search tree.

tools.name: BST inorder traversal
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to visit BST nodes in sorted order - with an inorder traversal.

tools.name: Parsing
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to parse simple, structured text into syntax trees and evaluate those trees.

tools.name: Working with adjacency lists
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use adjacency lists effectively and reason about their properties.

tools.name: Edge list to adjacency list conversion
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to convert an edge list into an adjacency list for graph problems; necessary for most graph problems.

tools.name: Graph DFS
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to solve graph connectivity problems by using DFS.

tools.name: Cycle detection (graphs)
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to detect cycles (or confirm acyclicity) in graphs using DFS or BFS.

tools.name: Graph BFS
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to solve graph problems about connectivity or distances by using BFS.

tools.name: Path reconstruction
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to reconstruct the sequence of nodes in a path in a graph, rather than just finding its length.

tools.name: DFS/BFS tree
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to compute and use a DFS or BFS tree.

tools.name: Connected-component loop
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to launch a BFS or DFS for each connected component by using a shared visited set.

tools.name: Multisource BFS
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to run BFS from multiple sources simultaneously.

tools.name: Working with grid graphs
tools.wanted_outcome: Being comfortable with problems where the input is a grid representing a graph, where the nodes are grid cells and adjacent cells are neighbors.

tools.name: Grid DFS
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to solve grid-graph connectivity problems by using DFS.

tools.name: Grid BFS
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to solve graph problems about connectivity or distances, when the graph is a grid graph, by using BFS.

tools.name: Graph modeling
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to reframe problems where the input is not a graph as questions about an inherent graph.

tools.name: Strong connectivity check
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to check strong connectivity in a directed graph.

tools.name: Working with weighted graphs
tools.wanted_outcome: Being comfortable working with graphs that have weighted nodes or edges.

tools.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to use Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path in a weighted graph.

tools.name: Minimum spanning tree
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing the MST definition and how to compute it using either Kruskal's or Prim's algorithm.

tools.name: Tinkering with heap internals
other_problems: K Smallest Elements in a Min-Heap
tools.wanted_outcome: Understanding heaps: the binary tree visualization, the array-based representation, the heap property, bubbling up/down, and linear-time heapify.

tools.name: Tracking smallest/largest elements
other_problems: Sum Of First K Prime Powers
tools.wanted_outcome: Tracking the smallest/largest elements in an evolving collection - with a heap.

tools.name: Heap size restriction
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to track the largest k elements in a collection with a min-heap of max size k, or vice versa.

tools.name: Two-heap median tracking
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to maintain the median of a stream using two heaps.

tools.name: Working with dynamic priorities in heaps
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to handle priority updates for elements already in the heap - usually via lazy deletion.

tools.name: M-way merge
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to merge M sorted lists efficiently with a heap.

tools.name: Fixed-length window recipe
tools.wanted_outcome: Having a recipe for sliding window problems where the window length is fixed.

tools.name: Resetting window recipe
other_problems: Count Good Subarrays With At Least K Sales
tools.wanted_outcome: Having a sliding-window recipe for cases where a single element can invalidate the entire window.

tools.name: Maximum window recipe
tools.primary_problem: Maximum With At Most 3 Bad Days
other_problems: Longest Period At Most K Distinct
tools.wanted_outcome: Having a sliding-window recipe for maximization problems.

tools.name: Reframing choices as optimization
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to reframe problems about making choices as optimization problems (when possible).

tools.name: Minimum window recipe
tools.wanted_outcome: Having a sliding-window recipe for minimization problems.

tools.name: At-most-k subarray counting
tools.primary_problem: Count Subarrays With At Most K Bad Days
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to count subarrays that satisfy an at-most-K condition - by using a sliding window.

tools.name: Transform exactly-k/at-least-k counting to at-most-k counting
other_problems: Count Subarrays With Exactly K Bad Days
Count Subarrays With At Least K Bad Days
Count Subarrays With Bad Days In Range
Count Good Subarrays With At Least K Sales
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve Exactly-K and At-Least-K subarray counting problems - by reducing it to the At-Most-K version.

tools.name: Backtracking recipe
other_problems: To Be or Not to Be
Count Unique Submultisets With Sum Zero
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to model problems as decision trees and traverse the implicit decision tree with DFS.

tools.name: Modify -> recurse -> undo
other_problems: To Be or Not to Be
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to optimize backtracking by reusing state across recursive calls - this involves modifying the state right before the recursive call and undoing the modification right after.

tools.name: Listing subsets
other_problems: To Be or Not to Be
Count Unique Submultisets With Sum Zero
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to generate all subsets via backtracking.

tools.name: Listing permutations
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to generate all permutations via backtracking.

tools.name: Identifying knapsack-type problems
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to recognize and solve knapsack-style problems with backtracking.

tools.name: Preventing cycles in backtracking
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to avoid infinite loops in backtracking by tracking visited positions.

tools.name: Choosing better decision trees
other_problems: Count Unique Submultisets With Sum Zero
tools.wanted_outcome: When modeling a problem as a decision tree for backtracking, being able to look for the smallest one.

tools.name: Memoization/Tabulation recipe
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve DP problems - by deriving a recurrence and then turning it into code with either memoization or tabulation (knowing one is usually enough); for example, memoization involves turning the recurrence into recursive code and then adding a memo map to avoid recomputation.

tools.name: DP with a non-constant number of choices
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to formulate DP recurrences when the number of choices at each subproblem is not constant.

tools.name: 2D DP
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to formulate and implement DP recurrences where the subproblems are identified by two (or more) parameters.

tools.name: Solution reconstruction
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to reconstruct a DP solution, not just compute its value.

tools.name: Formulating a greedy choice
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to develop greedy algorithms by brainstorming for greedy choices, hunting for counterexamples to your greedy choice, giving an intuitive explanation for why it works if you can't find any, and finally implementing it.

tools.name: Peel-off algorithm
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to compute a topological order (or find a cycle) by repeatedly removing nodes with in-degree zero (Kahn's algorithm).

tools.name: DAG problem recipe
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to solve typical DAG problems, like finding min distances, max distances, or path counts, by processing the nodes in topological order.

tools.name: Prefix-sum array
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to precompute prefix sums - usually to compute subarray sums quickly.

tools.name: Prefix-sum array for range sum queries
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to precompute prefix sums and then use that to quickly compute subarray sums.

tools.name: Custom prefix-sum array
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to precompute a prefix-sum-style (or suffix) array that incrementally computes custom aggregates beyond sums.

tools.name: Finding subarrays with sum k
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use prefix sums (and a hash map) to find or count subarrays that sum to k, including when negatives are present.

tools.name: Difference array
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to do range updates efficiently with a difference array.

tools.name: Tinkering with hash set internals
tools.wanted_outcome: Understanding hashing, collision handling, dynamic resizing, and probabilistic big O in hash sets.

tools.name: Tinkering with hash map internals
tools.wanted_outcome: Understanding hashing, collision handling, dynamic resizing, and probabilistic big O analysis in hash maps.

tools.name: Max queue
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement and use a queue that also returns the current max in the queue.

tools.name: Next greater element
other_problems: King Kong Vs Godzilla In The Fog
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to use a monotonic stack to find the next greater element for each array position.

tools.name: Tinkering with union-find internals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement an efficient union-find class with the path compression optimization, and - ideally - also union by size.

tools.name: Tracking connected components in evolving graphs
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to solve problems about graphs where edges are added over time - usually by using union-find to track the connected components.

tools.name: Implementing tries
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement a trie class that supports insert, search, and delete operations.

tools.name: Trie traversals
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to traverse a trie to find words matching a given pattern.

tools.name: Computing averages
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to simplify average computations by tracking the sum and count separately and dividing at the end.

tools.name: Distance formula (geometry)
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to compute Euclidean distances between points in the plane.

tools.name: Number of subarrays formula
tools.primary_problem: Count Subarrays With Good Start And Ending
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use the formula for the number of subarrays in an array.

tools.name: Extracting and combining digits
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to split a number into digits and combine digits back into a number.

tools.name: Applying modulo at each step
other_problems: Sum Of First K Prime Powers
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to prevent overflow by applying modulo operations throughout computations.

tools.name: Working with primes
tools.primary_problem: Sum Of First K Prime Powers
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use prime number properties in solutions.

tools.name: Closed formula
other_problems: Count Subarrays With Good Start And Ending
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to derive a closed-form formula that directly computes the result when one exists.

tools.name: Line-sweep algorithm
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to implement a line-sweep algorithm - sorting geometric elements by one coordinate and processing them in order.

tools.name: Brute force optimization: preprocessing pattern
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to speed up a brute-force solution by identifying what information is needed in the bottleneck and precomputing it in a preprocessing step (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Brute force optimization: data structure pattern
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to speed up a brute-force solution by identifying the key operations in the bottleneck and then asking which data structure is designed to speed up those operations (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Brute force optimization: skip unnecessary work
other_problems: Count Unique Submultisets With Sum Zero
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to identify and eliminate redundant work (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Transforming the input
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to preprocess the input into a more convenient format, as long as it doesn't change the big O analysis.

tools.name: Hunting for properties
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to stop to search for additional properties about the problem (instead of thinking about algorithms) that could be useful in the design of algorithms (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: DIY technique
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to use the DIY technique to come up with new solutions: taking a non-trivial example, solving it by hand (without thinking about algorithms), and then trying to reverse engineer the shortcuts your brain followed (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Case analysis
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to classify inputs into cases that are easier to handle separately (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Reverse engineering the output pattern
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to compute the outputs for the few first inputs manually to deduce the formula that yields those outputs (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Sketching a diagram
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing how to visualize the problem to reveal useful properties (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Reframing the problem
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to reformulate the problem in an equivalent way where the right approach is more apparent (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Starting with an easier version
tools.wanted_outcome: Knowing when to take a step back, starting with a simpler version to get the ball rolling, and then trying to adapt the approach to the original problem (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Breaking down the problem
tools.wanted_outcome: Being able to decompose a hard problem into manageable steps (general problem-solving technique).

tools.name: Cycling through the DSA catalog
tools.wanted_outcome: When stuck, being able to quickly scan standard DS&A techniques to see what might apply (general problem-solving technique).
